content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 28, 2004

Michelle Le
UST Training Group
P.O. Box 581241
Elk Grove, CA 95758

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-04-259

Dear Ms. Le:

This letter is in response to your request on for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your request seeks
general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTION

As a Registered Environmental Health Specialist with the San Joaquin County
Environmental Health Department whose job duties include inspection of underground
storage tank systems, would you have a conflict of interest under the Act by accepting
employment as a "designated operator" for underground storage tank facilities in other
counties?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit public officials from maintaining outside employment
during their tenure as public officials. However, any income received may be subject to
the reporting and conflict of interest provisions of the Act.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)

File No. I-04-259
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are currently employed as a Registered Environmental Health Specialist with
the San Joaquin County Environmental Health Department ("SJCEHD"). Your job
duties include inspections of underground storage tank systems ("UST's").

A new California regulation requires all UST facilities to obtain a designated
operator by January 1, 2005. A designated operator is an individual who has passed the
California UST System Operator exam, which is administered by the International Code
Counsel. The designated operator responsibilities include performing a monthly UST
inspection, verifying annual testing is done on time, and annually training of facility
personnel. UST owners have the option of becoming the designated operator themselves
or hiring an outside company.

You would like to offer your services as a designated operator in counties other
than the one in which you are employed. You would not perform the role of designated
operator for anyone who owns facilities in the county in which you are employed.

The State Water Resources Control Board ("SWRCB") has a website posting the
names and contact information for persons offering their services as a designated
operator. SWRCB will not add an individual to this posting who is employed as an
inspector until a "written notice" is received from the Commission stating that a conflict
of interest does not exist.

You have attached the memorandum from the SWRCB to local agencies
informing "local agency staff" of the potential for conflicts of interest should they also
provide services as designated UST operators. Additionally, the memorandum states the
SWRCB's requirement that SWRCB will not add a local agency staff person to its list of
available designated UST operators unless it receives "a written determination from the
FPPC stating that there is no conflict of interest between that individual's performing his
or her job duties as a local agency staff and his or her job duties as a Designated UST
Operator."

ANALYSIS

The Act does not bar public officials from maintaining outside employment
during their tenure as public officials.³ (Evans Advice Letter, No. I-01-143). However,
the Act does require public officials to report, in certain circumstances, any sources of
outside income.⁴ (Section 87302, regulation 18730.) Additionally, the Act's conflict-of-
interest provisions prohibit any public official from making, participating in making, or

___________________________
³ Our advice in this letter is limited to the provisions of the Political Reform Act. There may be
other restrictions based on outside employment under any incompatible activities policy adopted by your
agency. The Commission cannot offer advice in that area. Any questions regarding incompatible activities
should be addressed by the San Joaquin County Counsel.
⁴ Designated employees under an agency's conflict of interest code are required to report each
source of income aggregating five hundred dollars or more in value, from certain identified sources, and a
general description of the business activity of each source. (Section 87207.)

File No. I-04-259
Page No. 3

otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest. (Section 87100, regulation 18700.)

A public official has a disqualifying financial interest in a governmental decision if the
plain meaning of section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material effect on one or more of the official's economic interests. The
economic interests that may give rise to a potential conflict of interest are defined in
section 87103 and regulations 18703 through 18703.5 and include any source of income,
including promised income, to the public official which aggregates to $500 or more
within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c), regulation 18703.3.) Since
your inquiry is limited to potential conflicts of interest arising from outside income, and
you have not identified any other potential economic interests, our discussion is limited to
potential conflicts of interests arising from a source of income economic interest.

Section 82030(a) provides:

"(a) 'Income' means ... a payment received, including but not
limited to any salary, wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent,
proceeds from any sale, gift, including any gift of food or
beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received
by the filer, reimbursement for expenses, per diem, or contribution
to an insurance or pension program paid by any person other than
an employer, and including any community property interest in the
income of a spouse. Income also includes an outstanding loan.
Income of an individual also includes a pro rata share of any
income of any business entity or trust in which the individual or
spouse owns, directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 10-percent
interest or greater. 'Income,' other than a gift, does not include
income received from any source outside the jurisdiction and not
doing business within the jurisdiction, not planning to do business
within the jurisdiction, or not having done business within the
jurisdiction during the two years prior to the time any statement or
other action is required under this title." (Emphasis added.)

Section 82035 defines "jurisdiction" with respect to a local government agency as:
"the region, county, city, district or other geographical area in which it has jurisdiction."

Since you are employed by the County of San Joaquin, the jurisdiction applicable
to you is the county. You have indicated that you will only offer your services in
counties other than San Joaquin, and that you will not perform the role of designated
operator for anyone who owns facilities in the county in which you are employed. As
long as the source of your income is outside the county, does not do business or plan to
do business within the county, or has not done business within the county during the two
years prior to the time any governmental decision is made, the compensation you receive
from that source is not considered income from purposes of the conflict of interest
provisions of the Act.

File No. 1-04-259
Page No. 4

Since your request is a general one relating to prohibitions under the Act with
regard to accepting outside employment, you have not presented any facts pertaining to a
specific governmental decision or to a specific source of income that may cause a
potential conflict of interest. If you find that such a situation may be presented you may,
of course, seek additional advice based on those facts.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William J. Lenkeit
Counsel, Legal Division
